page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4n server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs. The instance is equipped with 124.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 7.75 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server configuration does not include bundled local storage or integrated graphics processing units, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network configuration or NAT gateways for external connectivity.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4n-highmem-16 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute or GPU acceleration. It is designed for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the c4n family, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 124.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation provides a high density of 7.75 GB of RAM per core, positioning the instance for memory-intensive workloads. The server does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the c4n-highmem-16 offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the overhead of excessive compute cores. It is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, data processing pipelines, and enterprise application servers.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring sixteen dedicated cores and a high memory-to-CPU ratio for memory-intensive enterprise applications.
